_comment: Hand-maintained. This is the ONLY file where humans/agents record known issues per capability. Do not duplicate this information in prose docs — STATUS.md is generated FROM this file plus recon_findings.json, so editing STATUS.md directly will be overwritten. Capability names must match keys in recon_findings.json exactly.

_schema.capability: must match a key in recon_findings.json capabilities{}
_schema.detail_ref: path#anchor to the full writeup, e.g. docs/adr/0003-xxx.md or docs/handoff/2026-07-03-audit-findings.md
_schema.blocks_goal: true if this blocks the project's primary use case (agent-driven strategy iteration)

issues.summary: Returns raw dialog text split by newline, not structured fields. No selector to target individual inputs.
Returns unparsed raw text blob instead of key/value pairs.
Uses guessed generic selectors (input[name=], [data-name=], placeholder match) never confirmed against real TradingView Inputs dialog DOM. Will likely silently fail to match.

Fixed: now uses CGEventPostToPid for paste (ADR-0006). Requires macOS Accessibility permission (AXIsProcessTrusted). Write verified via base64 clipboard + CGEventPostToPid Cmd+A+V, compile via JS click on button[title="Add to chart"]. Full round-trip confirmed 2026-07-03. compile timing may need tuning (delay after paste before compile).
Fixed: now uses Page.bringToFront + CGEventPostToPid Cmd+A+C (ADR-0006). Read round-trip confirmed 2026-07-03 (118 chars read back correctly). Requires macOS Accessibility permission.
Fixed: now uses base64 clipboard + Page.bringToFront + CGEventPostToPid Cmd+A+V (ADR-0006). Write verified 2026-07-03 (4-line script round-tripped). Requires macOS Accessibility permission.

Dead end on both implemented paths. DOM backend punts to network path with a CapabilityUnavailable; network backend's get_ohlcv also unconditionally raises CapabilityUnavailable despite its own docstring claiming OHLCV is the one thing the network path supports. No working OHLCV read exists.

Dropdown (combobox) writes confirmed working via backend.write() on Source field (Open→High). Checkbox writes NOT YET VERIFIED. The dialog selector div[data-name="indicator-properties-dialog"] in recon_findings.json is STALE — this element does not exist in TradingView Desktop 3.2.0's DOM. The actual settings dialog is div[data-name="series-properties-dialog"] (w=750,h=1130), opened via [data-qa-id="legend-settings-action"]. However, this dialog shows chart-level settings (Symbol, Status line, Scales, Canvas, Trading, Alerts, Events tabs) — NOT indicator Inputs/Style tabs. The indicator-specific properties dialog (with Inputs/Style/Visibility tabs and checkbox fields like plot visibility) was not found. Needs: (a) recon to find the correct dialog selector for indicator-specific settings in TV 3.2.0, or (b) confirmation that indicator inputs are only accessible via a different UI path (Pine Editor toolbar? right-click context menu?). Blocked until the real dialog is identified.
